title: Early T-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia with molecular failure in a 21-year-old female

patient.chief_complaint: Post-induction consultation for T-ALL with prior sinus vein thrombosis and intracerebral hemorrhage
patient.vital_signs: Blood pressure 122/74 mmHg, heart rate 96/min regular, respiratory rate 15/min, temperature 37.3 °C, oxygen saturation 97% on room air.

1.capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient after completion of induction therapy. You can review history and examination findings, request routine laboratory evaluations, and arrange standard imaging and specialist consultations appropriate to an ambulatory setting. More invasive procedures and specialized analyses usually require additional planning.

2.capabilities: The patient is undergoing structured follow-up after induction and consolidation. You can organize marrow-based assessments, extended evaluations by consulting services, and additional imaging. Some specialized test results may still be pending.

3.setting: Specialist conference focusing on MRD status and transplant planning
3.capabilities: You now have access to specialized molecular assessments, measurable residual disease data, and a detailed treatment history. You are expected to integrate these findings with donor availability and comorbidities to define prognosis and formulate a definitive strategy, including potential allogeneic transplantation or trial participation.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for age, sex, performance status, comorbidities, social situation, or anthropometric data.
demographics.content:
Age: 21 years
Sex: Female
Performance status: ECOG 0
HCT-CI Score: 1
Comorbidities: Hypothyroidism, endometriosis, sinus vein thrombosis with secondary intracerebral hemorrhage.
Social: Lives with parents, currently studying psychology
Height: 164 cm; Bodyweight: 82 kg

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for history of present illness, review of systems, physical examination, or current medications.
history_physical.content:
2025-06-29: The patient presents following completion of induction chemotherapy for T-ALL, seeking discussion regarding further treatment options. During induction, she developed a sinus vein thrombosis complicated by a small left-hemispheric intracerebral hemorrhage. Neurological sequelae include mild right arm weakness and impaired fine motor skills, for which neurological rehabilitation is planned. She remains uncertain about continuing intensive therapy and expresses consideration of treatment discontinuation. On examination, her general condition is good; she is alert, oriented, and cooperative. Skin and mucous membranes are pale. Several puncture sites on the left hand show minor erythema and superficial inflammation, consistent with previous peripheral venous catheter insertions. No palpable lymphadenopathy or hepatosplenomegaly. Cardiopulmonary examination: Normal heart sounds, no murmurs; lungs clear to auscultation. Abdomen soft and non-tender. Neurological examination: Cranial nerves intact; mild weakness and reduced grip strength in the right arm, otherwise full strength with normal reflexes, preserved coordination, and intact sensation.
Current medication: Levothyroxine 75 µg 1-0-0, leuprorelin monthly, enoxaparin 40 mg s.c. q12h, co-trimoxazole 960 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D supplementation. Self-medication with selenium and omega-3 fatty acids.

cbc_chemistry.valid_request_if: Requests for blood counts, coagulation parameters, or serum chemistry including liver and renal function.
cbc_chemistry.content:
Hb 9.4 g/dL, WBC 3.3 ×10⁹/L, Plt 122 ×10⁹/L; ANC 2.4 ×10⁹/L, lymphocytes 0.6 ×10⁹/L, monocytes 0.3 ×10⁹/L; no peripheral blasts detected.
Coagulation: INR 1.4, aPTT 31 sec, D-dimer 1850 µg/L FEU.
Na 137 mmol/L, K 3.7 mmol/L, creatinine 1.1 mg/dL (eGFR 72 mL/min/1.73 m²), AST 86 U/L, ALT 91 U/L, bilirubin 1.2 mg/dL, LDH 285 U/L, uric acid 5.6 mg/dL.

imaging_abdomen.valid_request_if: Requests for abdominal ultrasound or sonographic evaluation of liver and spleen.
imaging_abdomen.content: Abdominal ultrasound: Hepatomegaly (17.8 cm) with increased echogenicity consistent with hepatic steatosis, homogeneous parenchyma, no focal lesions. Spleen and kidneys of normal size and morphology, no hydronephrosis.

ct_thorax_abdomen.valid_request_if: Requests for CT imaging of chest and abdomen or staging imaging.
ct_thorax_abdomen.content: CT scan: No pathologic lymphadenopathy. Mild residual thymic tissue consistent with rebound hyperplasia in the anterior mediastinum. Lungs clear, no infiltrates or nodules. No organomegaly apart from hepatomegaly.

echocardiogram.valid_request_if: Requests for echocardiography or assessment of cardiac function.
echocardiogram.content: Echocardiogram: Normal chamber dimensions and preserved systolic function (LVEF 61%), no wall motion abnormalities, valves structurally and functionally normal, no pericardial effusion.

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for bone marrow morphology and flow cytometry findings at post-induction evaluation.
bone_marrow.content: The bone marrow was normocellular with increased erythropoiesis and granulopoiesis, and megakaryopoiesis showing occasional juvenile forms. There was no excess of blasts or plasma cells, and no morphologic evidence of relapse, dysplasia, or fibrosis. Flow cytometry demonstrated a reduced B-cell compartment with a predominantly immature phenotype (CD10⁺/CD19⁺). Granulopoiesis appeared relatively increased with a normal maturation pattern. Approximately 2% CD34⁺/CD33⁺ myeloid blasts were detected, without evidence of lymphoid blasts or aberrant antigen expression. Initial immunophenotype at first diagnosis was: ETP-ALL: CD7+, CD34+, CD38+, TdT+, partly positive for CD33+ and CD117+ no CD1a, sCD3-, CD5 or CD8.

cytogenetics.valid_request_if: Requests for karyotype or conventional cytogenetic results.

extended_diagnostics.valid_request_if: Requests for extended evaluations such as ENT, gynecologic, ophthalmologic, thyroid, dental, infection screen, immunology, cardiology, pulmonary function, CSF analysis, or brain imaging.
extended_diagnostics.content:
ENT evaluation: Normal findings; no sinus or airway pathology. Gynecologic examination: Unremarkable; Pap smear negative for intraepithelial lesion or malignancy, HPV PCR negative. Ophthalmology: Normal ocular status, no retinopathy or CNS involvement. Thyroid ultrasound: Homogeneous parenchyma, no nodules, and normal vascularity. Dental status: Intact, no caries or chronic infectious focus identified.
Infection screen: Serum galactomannan <0.5; β-D-glucan not performed. CMV PCR negative. Rectal swab: Colonization with ESBL-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae (OXA-48 carbapenemase); MRSA negative, VRE positive. Immunology: ELISPOT negative.
Cardiology: ECG demonstrated normal sinus rhythm. Pulmonary function testing: Normal diffusion capacity (DLCO 88% predicted) and FEV₁ 91% predicted.
CSF analysis: Clear fluid, no elevated cell count, no malignant cells or blasts detected.
Brain MRI (06/15/2025): Demonstrated a small left parietal post-hemorrhagic lesion with hemosiderin deposition, consistent with residual changes following prior intracerebral hemorrhage. No acute bleeding, no new ischemic or demyelinating lesions, and no evidence of CNS leukemic involvement or meningeosis. Ventricular system and midline structures unremarkable.

molecular_panel.valid_request_if: Requests for molecular or NGS findings including rearrangements and mutations.
molecular_panel.content: Persistent IGH D1–7 J4 rearrangement and TCR D2–D3 rearrangement detectable at a ratio of 1E-01. NRAS mutation present.

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for measurable residual disease status and markers used for MRD monitoring.
mrd_status.content: MRD positive (0.1%). IGH D1–7 J4 and TCR D2–D3 rearrangements. NRAS mutation.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for detailed prior leukemia therapy, complications, CNS prophylaxis, and consolidation regimen.
treatment_history.content:
2025-03-02: The patient was newly diagnosed with early T-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ETP-ALL). A pre-phase regimen was initiated consisting of dexamethasone 10 mg/m² p.o. daily for 5 days and cyclophosphamide 200 mg/m² i.v. for 3 days, with good tolerability and rapid improvement of constitutional symptoms. A donor search was initiated, and an HLA-matched sibling was identified as a potential stem cell donor.
2025-03-10: Induction chemotherapy was started according to a standard adult ALL protocol. Dexamethasone p.o. 10 mg/m² (20 mg/day, divided into three doses) administered on days 6–7 and 13–16. Daunorubicin 45 mg/m² (86 mg i.v.) on days 6–7 and 13–14. Vincristine 2 mg i.v. (absolute dose) on days 6, 13, 20. PEG-asparaginase 500 U/m² (950 U absolute) on day 20. The regimen was tolerated initially; however, on day 17, the patient developed febrile neutropenia with Escherichia coli bacteremia, requiring intravenous meropenem 1 g q8h and short-course vancomycin (1.5 g q12h x 3 days) until cultures cleared. No hemodynamic instability occurred. Mild hyperglycemia was noted under steroids, managed conservatively.
2025-03-31: Bone marrow evaluation demonstrated blasts < 5%, consistent with hematologic remission. Intrathecal methotrexate 15 mg was administered on days 25, 32, and 39 as CNS prophylaxis. 6-Mercaptopurine 60 mg/m² (110 mg/day p.o.) was given on days 31–44. Cyclophosphamide 1000 mg/m² (1900 mg i.v.) was administered on days 24 and 44. Cytarabine 75 mg/m² (140 mg i.v.) was administered on days 26–29, 33–36, and 40–43. The patient experienced transient grade 2 mucositis and fatigue, but no neurotoxicity.
2025-04-14: CNS prophylaxis with cranial irradiation (24 Gy in fractionated doses) was initiated without acute toxicity.
2025-05-02: Bone marrow puncture after Induction I confirmed ongoing hematologic complete remission, pending molecular MRD assessment. 2025-05-14: The patient was discharged home in stable clinical condition.
2025-05-18: The patient was readmitted due to sudden severe headache and nausea. Cranial MRI revealed cerebral sinus venous thrombosis (superior sagittal sinus) with a small left-hemispheric intracerebral hemorrhage. She was admitted to the stroke unit for monitoring and received therapeutic-dose low-molecular-weight heparin (enoxaparin 90mg q12h s.c.). Neurological symptoms improved gradually, leaving a mild residual weakness of the right arm. PEG-asparaginase therapy was considered the most likely contributing factor to the thrombotic event.
2025-05-27: After multidisciplinary discussion, consolidation chemotherapy was initiated with modifications to reduce CNS risk. Treatment consisted of: Dexamethasone 10 mg/m² (20 mg/day p.o., divided) on days 61–65. Vindesine 5mg on day 61. Methotrexate 1500 mg/m² (2850 mg i.v.) on day 61 with standard hydration and folinic acid rescue. Etoposide 250 mg/m² (475 mg i.v.) on days 64–65. Cytarabine 2000 mg/m² (3800 mg i.v. twice daily) on day 65. Intrathecal triple prophylaxis (methotrexate + cytarabine + hydrocortisone) was omitted due to the history of sinus venous thrombosis and prior intracerebral hemorrhage. The course was complicated by cytopenia (transfusion dependent) and grade 2 increase of liver entymes, but no infectious events or neurologic deterioration.
The patient was discharged for follow-up and neurological rehabilitation, with continuation of therapeutic anticoagulation and infection prophylaxis (valaciclovir, co-trimoxazole).

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a concise description of the current leukemia status.
disease_status.content: Early T-cell precursor lymphoblastic leukemia, molecular failure.

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for special considerations that influence therapeutic decisions, including neurologic complications, performance status, and patient preferences.
special_considerations.content: Young patient with early T-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia in hematologic complete remission following induction chemotherapy, yet with persistently MRD positivity. The clinical course was complicated by cerebral sinus venous thrombosis with a small left-hemispheric intracerebral hemorrhage. Current performance status remains favorable (ECOG 0). Neurologically, the patient exhibits only mild residual weakness and reduced fine motor function of the right arm. She expresses hesitation to proceed with further intensive therapy and is considering temporary interruption of oncologic treatment in favor of neurological rehabilitation. Bone marrow reserve is preserved, organ function remains stable, and there is no clinical or laboratory evidence of active infection at present.

treatment_plan.action: Bone marrow aspirate for remission status and MRD assessment
Maintain enoxaparin 40 mg s.c. q12h for sinus vein thrombosis
Monitoring sinus vein thrombosis with MRI and neurological exams
Monitor bleeding risk (platelets, INR, aPTT, neurological exams)
Obtain CRP and PCT, continue monitoring temperature for infection surveillance

key_findings_quotes: The patient presents following completion of induction chemotherapy for T-ALL, seeking discussion regarding further treatment options
Sinus vein thrombosis complicated by a small left-hemispheric intracerebral hemorrhage
Mild right arm weakness and impaired fine motor skills, for which neurological rehabilitation is planned
Skin and mucous membranes are pale; Hb 9.4 g/dL
Several puncture sites on the left hand show minor erythema and superficial inflammation
Hepatomegaly (17.8 cm) with increased echogenicity consistent with hepatic steatosis

round_2.working_diagnosis: Early T-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ETP-ALL)

treatment_plan.action: Continue anticoagulation at current therapeutic dose
Adjust consolidation chemotherapy regimen to reduce CNS risk
Obtain antibiogram for ESBL-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae colonization

key_findings_quotes: Initial immunophenotype at first diagnosis was: ETP-ALL: CD7+, CD34+, CD38+, TdT+, partly positive for CD33+ and CD117+ no CD1a, sCD3-, CD5 or CD8
Rectal swab: Colonization with ESBL-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae (OXA-48 carbapenemase); MRSA negative, VRE positive

Brain MRI: Demonstrated a small left parietal post-hemorrhagic lesion with hemosiderin deposition, consistent with residual changes following prior intracerebral hemorrhage. No acute bleeding, no new ischemic or demyelinating lesions
No evidence of CNS leukemic involvement or meningeosis
CSF analysis: Clear fluid, no elevated cell count, no malignant cells or blasts detected

round_3.working_diagnosis: Early T-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia, molecular failure

treatment_plan.action: Consider enrollment in clinical trial evaluating CD38-targeted therapy (isatuximab or daratumumab) to achieve deeper molecular remission prior to transplantation
Proceed to myeloablative allogeneic HSCT from HLA-matched sibling donor with TBI 12 Gy, etoposide 60 mg/kg, and ATG conditioning upon adequate MRD response
GvHD prophylaxis with cyclosporine and MMF
Present case to transplant board for multidisciplinary planning
Continue therapeutic anticoagulation and infection prophylaxis (valaciclovir, co-trimoxazole)

key_findings_quotes: MRD positive (0.1%). IGH D1-7 J4 and TCR D2-D3 rearrangements. NRAS mutation
Persistent IGH D1-7 J4 rearrangement and TCR D2-D3 rearrangement detectable at a ratio of 1E-01
HLA-matched sibling was identified as a potential stem cell donor
Intrathecal triple prophylaxis was omitted due to the history of sinus venous thrombosis and prior intracerebral hemorrhage
Given persistent MRD positivity after induction and consolidation, the patient should be considered for enrollment in a clinical trial evaluating CD38-targeted therapy

Proceed to myeloablative allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT) from the HLA-matched sibling donor, using conditioning with total body irradiation (TBI) 12 Gy, etoposide 60 mg/kg, and antithymocyte globulin (ATG)
